Table of contents

  • 1 Table and figure structure:
  • 2 Main tables
    • 2.1 Table 1 - general participant descriptives
    • 2.2 Table 2 - Main analysis
  • 3 Main figures
    • 3.1 Figure 1 - descriptives of risk factors with MD
      • 3.1.1 Reproductive, lifestyle and other risk factors
      • 3.1.2 Tumour characteristics
    • 3.2 Figure 2 - adjusting for MD
      • 3.2.1 Categorical MD (quartiles)
      • 3.2.2 Continuous MD
  • 4 Supplementary material
    • 4.1 ST1 - MD subset general descriptives
    • 4.2 ST 2 - Risk factor descriptives by density
    • 4.3 ST 3 - subset on cases with MD
      • 4.3.1 Categorical MD (quartiles)
      • 4.3.2 Continuous MD - scaled to 10% = 1 unit
    • 4.4 ST 4 - TC descriptives and association with detection mode
    • 4.5 ST 5 - Risk factor analysis adjusted for TC
    • 4.6 ST 6 - Differences in characteristics between those with and without mammographic density

Detection mode project: Tables and figures for publication

Author

Martina Brayley

Published

July 29, 2024

1 Table and figure structure:

Main tables

Table 1 - general descriptives (population characteristics)

Table 2 - Risk factor descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results (unadjusted for TC) - all cases

Main figures

Figure 1 - comprised figure of plots describing mamographic density in relation to risk factors (detail in ST 2)

Figure 2 - Forest plot of analysis adjusted vs unadjusted for mammographic density (full table in supplementary material - ST 2)

Supplementary material

(ST = Supplementary Table)

ST 1 - Descriptives for MD cases (same as table 1 but subset)

ST 2 - risk factor descriptives by density (complementing figure 1)

ST 3 - subset on cases with MD. Same model as table 3 + adjusted for MD. Include mediation measures (% change)

ST 4 - TC descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results

ST 5 - Risk factor analysis adjusted for tumour characteristics (sensitivity analysis) - on subset of cases with TC

2 Main tables

stage coding - DCIS coded as stage 0 - should we keep it that way or code them as unknown? or DCIS?

time from recruitement to diagnosis categorical or continuous?

2.2 Table 2 - Main analysis

The main analysis is a logistic regression with multiple exposures (risk factors).

This table shows risk factor level descriptives and their associations with the mode of detection (logistic regression) adjusted for all other risk factors, age at diagnosis and time from entry to BC diagnosis.

Model 2 is the final selected model (Default model) to present in the analysis. Model 2 is adjusted for all co-exposures + age at diagnosis and time from entry to BC diagnosis. Model 1 was only adjusted for co-exposures, e.i. all exposures in the model.

Because model 2 was selected as the default model, it is also used on the subanalyses with TC and MD as the comparison against adjusted models for TC and MD.

3.2.1.1 without reference categories

# 3. GENERATE FORESTPLOT

md_forest_long <- md_forest_long %>% 
  arrange(variable) %>% 
  group_by(mdl) %>% 
  mutate(mean = estimate,
         lower = conf.low,
         upper = conf.high,
         level_var = level,
         estimate6 = case_when(mdl == "mdl6" & !is.na(estimate) ~ as.character(estimate),
                               mdl == "mdl6" & is.na(estimate) ~ "-"),
         estimate8 = case_when(mdl == "mdl8" & !is.na(estimate) ~ as.character(estimate),
                               mdl == "mdl8" & is.na(estimate) ~ "-")
         ) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  fill(estimate6, .direction = "down") %>% 
  fill(estimate8, .direction = "up") 
  



# indicate which rows to be bold = summary


is_summary <- if_else(
  md_forest_long$level %in% c("Menopausal status", 
                               "Age at menopause", 
                               "HRT status",
                               "N of parous pregnancies",
                               "Age at first birth",
                               "Age at menarche",
                               "Oral contraceptives status",
                               "Benign breast disease",
                               "Family history of BC",
                               "Alcohol units",
                               "Smoking status",
                               "BMI",
                               "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                               "Mammographic breast density quartile"), TRUE, FALSE
)


# plot

md_forest_long %>% 
  group_by(mdl
           ) %>% 
  mutate(mean = estimate,
         lower = conf.low,
         upper = conf.high
         ) %>% 
  forestplot(labeltext = c(level, estimate6, estimate8),
             xlog = FALSE,
             xlab = "log OR",
             boxsize = 0.3,
             zero = 0,
             line.margin = 0.5,
             clip = c(-1, 5),
             ticks = c(-1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5),
             ci.vertices = T,
             ci.vertices.height = 0.2,
             legend = c("Default model", "Adjusted for MD"),
             title = paste("Final model adjusted vs unadjusted for mamographic breast density quartiles"),
             is.summary = is_summary
             ) %>% 
  fp_set_style(box = c("grey50","orangered2") %>%  lapply(function(x) gpar(fill = x, col = "#666666")),
               default = gpar(vertices = T),
               line = "grey40",
               summary = gpar(col = NA), # Set summary color to NA
               align = "lrrr",
               hrz_lines = gpar(col = NA), # Set line color to NA
               txt_gp = fpTxtGp(label = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    ticks = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    xlab = gpar(cex = 0.8)
                                )
               ) %>% 
  fp_decorate_graph(graph.pos = 2
                    ) %>% 
  fp_add_header(level = c("", "Risk factor"),
            #variable = c("", "Variable"),
            estimate6 = c("Estimate", "unadjusted for MD"),
            estimate8 = c("Estimate", "adjusted for MD")
                ) %>% 
  fp_insert_row(level = "",
                position = 3,
                mean = NULL,
                lower = NULL,
                upper = NULL,
                is.summary = TRUE,
                boxsize = NA)
